data.title: ClearSign Technologies regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• ClearSign Technologies (NASDAQ:CLIR) on Monday said it has received a Nasdaq notice, informing that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Enphase Energy collaborates with NextEnergy in the Netherlands
data.description:
• Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) has announced a collaboration with NextEnergy, one of the most innovative energy providers in the Netherlands.
  • This strategic relationship will enable NextEnergy customers with Enphase solar and battery systems to participate in the grid imbalance energy marketplace in the Netherlands, which can help maximize their return on investment and reduce the system payback period.
  • Enphase recently launched its IQ® Battery 5P and IQ® Energy Management software in the Netherlands, which enables homeowners to manage dynamic energy tariffs and can help maximize energy savings.
  • The collaboration now enables owners of Enphase IQ Batteries to participate in NextEnergy’s dynamic tariff program, as well as join its virtual power plant to help manage grid imbalances and unlock further compensation.
  • “Our innovative solar and battery systems paired with NextEnergy’s revolutionary approach to providing energy offer a win for homeowners, the grid, and all ratepayers across the Netherlands,” said Sabbas Daniel, senior vice president of sales at Enphase Energy.

data.title: HubSpot sees price target raised at Stifel amid bullish views after partner survey
data.description:
Stifel maintained its Buy rating on HubSpot (NYSE:HUBS) and raised the price target on the shares to $880 from $750 following a partner survey conducted by the firm.

Analysts led by J. Parker Lane said that 2024 has been a year of significant updates for HubSpot, including a redefined pricing model in March, the relaunch of CMS Hub as Content Hub in April, and some AI-related announcements throughout the year. Commentary from HubSpot and its partners has been largely positive around product updates and has trended in the right direction around the company's new pricing model.

The analysts added that they conducted a survey (n=26) over the past week to get a more quantitative look at recent changes.

"Survey results came in generally as expected, with partners noting excitement about new products, Content Hub and Breeze Agents/Intelligence specifically, and the ROI [return on investment] of the platform as a whole," said Lane and his team.

However, the analysts added that, room for improvement is apparent, most specifically within Commerce Hub. HubSpot's product remains best-in-class in software, considering the consistent positivity around its mission-critical nature and positive ROI, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that strong product development in recent years also gives reason to be optimistic about the sustainability of the platform's high standing.

In addition, Lane and his team said that heading into 2025, HubSpot remains one of their top picks, with its leading product standing and development being key components of their thesis.

The analysts believe the team in place today has proven their ability to extend the growth curve through consistent product portfolio refinement numerous times since being public. The analysts added that they continue to believe HubSpot's shares deserve to trade at a premium to mid-cap software peers.

HubSpot's shares were largely flat premarket on Monday.

data.title: LM Funding America signs warrant exercise agreement for gross proceeds of $5.1M
data.description:
• LM Funding America (NASDAQ:LMFA) on Monday announced it has signed a warrant exercise agreement with a single institutional investor for about 1.73M outstanding common stock warrants to purchase around 1.73M shares of common stock for cash at $2.98 per share.
  • The existing warrants were previously issued in a private placement which closed in August 2024.
  • In consideration for the immediate exercise of the existing warrants, the exercising holder received new unregistered common stock warrants to purchase 3.47M shares of common stock.
  • The new warrants will have an exercise price of $2.95 per share and will be immediately exercisable for a period of five years from the issuance date.
  • The gross proceeds of the exercise of the existing warrants to the company, before deducting estimated expenses and fees, are expected to be about $5.1M.
  • LMFA -4.74% premarket to $2.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Evolv Motorola Solutions appoints John Kedzierski as CEO
data.description:
• Evolv Technology (NASDAQ:EVLV) has appointed John Kedzierski as the Company’s new CEO, President and as a member of the Board, effective Dec. 16, 2024.
  • Mr. Kedzierski will succeed Michael Ellenbogen, who has been serving as the Co.’s Interim President and CEO since October.
  • Mr. Ellenbogen will continue to serve as the Co.’s Chief Innovation Officer and a member of the Board.

data.title: Achieve Life Sciences appoints Mark Oki as CFO
data.description:
• Achieve Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ACHV) said it has named Mark Oki as its CFO.
  • Oki has over 25 years of experience in financial leadership positions, and most recently, he served as the finance chief of Aytu BioPharma.

data.title: Perpetua Resources, United States Antimony ink formal testing agreement
data.description:
United States Antimony (NYSE:UAMY) +16.8% and Perpetua Resources (NASDAQ:PPTA) +6.9% pre-market Monday after announcing a formal metallurgical testing agreement to evaluate stibnite-rich material from Perpetua's Stibnite gold project in Idaho.

Perpetua (PPTA) said it will supply samples of antimony ore and assay work for evaluation at U.S. Antimony's (UAMY) Montana facility.

The companies said the testing will allow the evaluation of antimony product specifications that may be able to be commercially produced from Perpetua (PPTA) ore at U.S. Antimony's (UAMY) processing plant, but they have not entered into any joint agreements for commercial production at the current evaluation stage.

"The Stibnite gold project contains the largest near-term opportunity to secure American antimony and identifying domestic processing opportunities is important to our ability to supply antimony for America's defense, energy, and technology industries," Perpetua (PPTA) CEO Jon Cherry said.

Separately, Perpetua (PPTA) said it entered into a memorandum of understanding to explore antimony processing opportunities with Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining in Idaho.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile stock jumps on long-term deal with Vodafone
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) shares advanced around 9% Monday morning after it signed long-term commercial deal through 2034 with Vodafone Group (NASDAQ:VOD).

Vodafone (VOD) has been a three-time investor in AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) since 2018 and is a key technology partner in its development. This agreement establishes the framework for Vodafone to offer space-based cellular broadband connectivity in its home markets, as well as to other operators via its Partner Markets program.

The telecom firm has placed an order for its first Block 1 BlueBird gateway that will enable users outside traditional cellular coverage to connect their everyday smartphones directly to AST SpaceMobile's satellites in low Earth orbit, which in turn will route the data to the gateway.

These gateways will then connect to Vodafone's existing network infrastructure to route the broadband data to users' devices, as well as to access third-party Apps and the Internet

The deal marks a milestone in the deployment of AST SpaceMobile's (ASTS) global network.
